user.title: Connection Type
user.description: Select how your printer is connected.

data_description.connection_type: Choose Network for TCP/IP connected printers or USB for directly connected printers.

network.title: Configure network printer
network.description: Enter the connection details for your ESC/POS network printer.

data_description.host: IP address or hostname of the printer.
data_description.profile: Select your printer model or Auto-detect. Choose 'Custom' to enter a profile name manually.

usb_select.title: Configure USB printer
usb_select.description: Select your USB thermal printer from the list of detected devices.

data_description.usb_device: Select your printer from the detected USB devices.
data_description.profile: Select your printer model or Auto-detect.

usb_all_devices.title: Select USB device
usb_all_devices.description: Browse all connected USB devices. Note: Not all USB devices are thermal printers - select your printer from the list below.

data_description.usb_device: Select your printer from all connected USB devices.
data_description.in_ep: Leave at default (130) unless your printer doesn't work. This is the USB address for receiving status from the printer.
data_description.out_ep: Leave at default (1) unless your printer doesn't work. This is the USB address for sending data to the printer.
data_description.profile: Select your printer model or Auto-detect.

usb_manual.title: Manual USB configuration
usb_manual.description: Enter the USB Vendor ID and Product ID for your printer. These can be found using 'lsusb' on Linux or Device Manager on Windows.

data_description.vendor_id: USB Vendor ID (decimal, e.g., 1208 for Epson).
data_description.in_ep: Leave at default (130) unless your printer doesn't work. This is the USB address for receiving status from the printer.
data_description.out_ep: Leave at default (1) unless your printer doesn't work. This is the USB address for sending data to the printer.
data_description.profile: Select your printer model or Auto-detect.

usb_confirm.title: Confirm USB printer
usb_confirm.description: A USB thermal printer was detected: {printer_name}. Configure the printer profile.

data_description.profile: Select your printer model or Auto-detect.

bluetooth_select.title: Configure Bluetooth printer
bluetooth_select.description: Select a paired Bluetooth printer. If your printer isn't listed, pair it on the host first (HA OS: Settings → System → Hardware; otherwise run 'bluetoothctl pair AA:BB:CC:DD:EE:FF' on the host) and try again, or choose Manual MAC entry.

data_description.bt_device: Select your printer from the list of paired Bluetooth devices.
data_description.rfcomm_channel: RFCOMM channel (almost always 1 for ESC/POS printers; run 'bluetoothctl info [MAC]' on the host and look for the SerialPort service-record 'Channel:' line to confirm).
data_description.profile: Select your printer model or Auto-detect.

bluetooth_manual.title: Manual Bluetooth configuration
bluetooth_manual.description: Enter the Bluetooth MAC address of a printer you have already paired on the host (HA OS: Settings → System → Hardware; otherwise 'bluetoothctl pair [MAC]'). The integration only opens an RFCOMM socket — pairing must be done outside the integration.

data_description.bt_mac: Bluetooth MAC address in AA:BB:CC:DD:EE:FF format.
data_description.rfcomm_channel: RFCOMM channel (almost always 1 for ESC/POS printers).
data_description.profile: Select your printer model or Auto-detect.

bluetooth_no_devices.title: No paired Bluetooth printers found
bluetooth_no_devices.description:
Your Home Assistant host can't see any paired Bluetooth printers. Pair the printer first, then come back to this screen.

**On Home Assistant OS / Supervised**: open Settings → Devices & services → Bluetooth, put your printer into pairing mode, and accept the pairing.

**On Home Assistant Core or Container**: pair from the host shell:

```
bluetoothctl
[bluetooth]# scan on
[bluetooth]# pair AA:BB:CC:DD:EE:FF
[bluetooth]# trust AA:BB:CC:DD:EE:FF
```

If bluez D-Bus isn't reachable from this container (rootless Docker, no `/run/dbus` mount), use the **socat host bridge** described in the [setup guide]({docs_url}). Or if you already know the printer's MAC address, **submit this form** to enter it manually.

bluetooth_channel_retry.title: Try a different RFCOMM channel
bluetooth_channel_retry.description: The printer at {mac} refused the default RFCOMM channel (1). Most ESC/POS printers use channel 1 — your printer may use a different one. Run `bluetoothctl info [MAC]` on the host and look for the SerialPort service-record 'Channel:' line. If you don't have shell access, try 2 or 3.

data_description.rfcomm_channel: Channel number (1-30); see the printer's SDP service record.

codepage.title: Printer settings
codepage.description: Configure encoding and printing defaults for your printer.

data_description.codepage: Character encoding/codepage for text. Choose 'Custom' to enter a codepage manually.
data_description.line_width: Number of characters per line (columns). Choose 'Custom' to enter a value manually.
data_description.default_cut: Default paper cut mode after printing.

custom_profile.title: Custom printer profile
custom_profile.description: Enter the profile name as defined in escpos-printer-db.

data_description.custom_profile: The exact profile key from escpos-printer-db (e.g., 'TM-T88V', 'default').

custom_line_width.title: Custom line width
custom_line_width.description: Enter the number of characters per line.

data_description.custom_line_width: Number of characters that fit on one line (e.g., 32, 42, 48).

error.cannot_connect: Failed to connect to network printer.
error.cannot_connect_usb: Failed to connect to USB printer. Check that the printer is connected and permissions are configured correctly.
error.usb_permission_denied: Permission denied accessing USB device. On Linux, add udev rules or run with appropriate permissions. On Docker/HAOS, ensure the USB device is passed through to the container.
error.usb_kernel_driver_active: A kernel driver is active for this USB device. Stop other printing services or detach/blacklist the driver (e.g., usblp on Linux).
error.usb_device_busy: USB device is busy. Another application or driver may be using the printer.
error.usb_io_error: USB I/O error while opening the device. This can be transient (reset, autosuspend, or power issue). Try again or replug the printer.
error.usb_device_not_found: USB device not found. Verify the printer is connected and the Vendor ID/Product ID are correct.
error.usb_backend_missing: USB backend (libusb) not found. Install libusb on your system.
error.invalid_line_width: Invalid line width. Must be a positive number.
error.invalid_endpoint: Invalid USB endpoint address. Must be between 0x00 and 0xFF (0-255).
error.cannot_connect_bt: Failed to connect to Bluetooth printer. Verify the device is paired, powered on, and within range.
error.bt_unavailable: Bluetooth Classic (AF_BLUETOOTH/RFCOMM) is not available in this environment. On Home Assistant Container this typically means the container needs '--net=host' and the NET_ADMIN/NET_RAW capabilities. See the README for setup instructions.
error.bt_permission_denied: Permission denied opening the Bluetooth socket. Ensure the Home Assistant process can access bluez (typically requires the 'bluetooth' group on bare Linux).
error.bt_device_not_found: Bluetooth device not found. Pair the printer first using 'bluetoothctl' on the host or HA OS Settings → System → Hardware.
error.bt_host_down: Bluetooth printer is not reachable. Make sure it's powered on, within range, and not currently connected to another host (most BT printers accept only one connection at a time).
error.bt_timeout: Timed out connecting to Bluetooth printer. The printer may be asleep — try printing once to wake it, or increase the timeout.
error.bt_channel_refused: The printer is reachable but refused the RFCOMM channel. Most ESC/POS printers use channel 1 — try a different channel, or run 'bluetoothctl info [MAC]' on the host to confirm the SerialPort service-record channel.
error.invalid_bt_mac: Invalid Bluetooth MAC address. Expected format AA:BB:CC:DD:EE:FF.
error.invalid_rfcomm_channel: Invalid RFCOMM channel. Must be between 1 and 30.

abort.invalid_usb_device: Invalid USB device configuration. Vendor ID and Product ID are required.

init.title: Printer options
init.description: Configure printer settings. Changing the profile will reset encoding options to defaults.

data_description.profile: Select your printer model. Changing this will reset encoding options.
data_description.default_cut: Default paper cut mode after printing.
data_description.reliability_profile: Picks safe defaults for image chunk size, inter-chunk delay, and ESC/POS image command. Service-call options always override these.
data_description.keepalive: Keep the connection open between print jobs (network printers only).
data_description.status_interval: How often to check printer status (0 to disable).

custom_profile.title: Custom printer profile
custom_profile.description: Enter the profile name as defined in escpos-printer-db.

data_description.custom_profile: The exact profile key from escpos-printer-db.

custom_line_width.title: Custom line width
custom_line_width.description: Enter the number of characters per line.

data_description.custom_line_width: Number of characters that fit on one line.

print_text.name: Print Text (Raw Encoding)
print_text.description: Print text to the ESC/POS printer using raw encoding.

width.name: Width multiplier
width.description: Text width multiplier (normal/double/triple, or 1-8 in YAML).

height.name: Height multiplier
height.description: Text height multiplier (normal/double/triple, or 1-8 in YAML).

print_text_utf8.name: Print Text (UTF-8)
print_text_utf8.description: Print UTF-8 text with automatic transcoding to the printer's codepage. Converts special characters like curly quotes, em-dashes, and accented letters to their closest equivalents.

text.name: Text
text.description: UTF-8 text content to print. Special characters will be automatically converted.

width.name: Width multiplier
width.description: Text width multiplier (normal/double/triple, or 1-8 in YAML).

height.name: Height multiplier
height.description: Text height multiplier (normal/double/triple, or 1-8 in YAML).

data.name: Data
data.description: Data content to encode in the QR code.

size.name: Size
size.description: Module size for the QR code (1-16).

align.name: Alignment
align.description: Horizontal alignment for the QR code.

print_image.name: Print image
print_image.description: Print an image by URL or local path.

image.name: Image
image.description: Path or URL to the image to print.

high_density.name: High density
high_density.description: Use high density image mode if supported.

feed.name: Feed
feed.description: Feed the paper by a number of lines.

font.name: Font
font.description: Font for human-readable text (A or B).

align_ct.name: Center align
align_ct.description: Center the barcode on the line.

force_software.name: Force software renderer
force_software.description: Force software rendering mode to improve compatibility.

duration.name: Duration
duration.description: Duration of each beep in units supported by the printer.

print_message.name: Print Formatted Message
print_message.description: Print a message with full text formatting to an ESC/POS thermal printer.

title.name: Title
title.description: Optional title printed before the message.

width.name: Width multiplier
width.description: Text width multiplier (normal/double/triple, or 1-8 in YAML).

height.name: Height multiplier
height.description: Text height multiplier (normal/double/triple, or 1-8 in YAML).

utf8.name: UTF-8 mode
utf8.description: Enable UTF-8 transcoding to automatically convert special characters to printer-compatible encoding.

encoding.name: Encoding
encoding.description: Character encoding/codepage for text (ignored when UTF-8 mode is enabled).

profile_width_fallback.title: Printer profile is missing the pixel width
profile_width_fallback.description:
The selected printer profile ({profile}) does not expose `media.width.pixels`, so images are being scaled to a generic {fallback}px width. This is usually correct for 58 mm thermals but may print stretched or truncated on 80 mm printers.

**To fix:** open the integration's options, pick the closest matching printer profile, or set `image_width` explicitly in your service calls. If your printer's profile name is correct, the python-escpos profile database may need updating.
